eros-day.summary: The flagship skill. 9 phases, 23 sections in final report, 35 active sites tracked. Auto-execute on. Mature.
whatWorks: 9 phases run idempotently — safe to re-run any phase same day
Auto-execute eliminates the friction of manual approvals at each phase
Final report Section #24 mirrors full /bail-reports detail (added today)
Phase 1 step 5 — Semrush LHF integration gives content direction (added today)
Tier-driven triage (S/A/B/C/D) prevents wasting effort on wrong sites
Operator-notes hook (Phase 0 step 10) closes the dashboard improvement loop
whatDoesntWork: No external duplicate-content check today — internal Jaccard catches microsite-vs-microsite, but the 245-blog backlog has 126 below 70% unique (just discovered)
Fact-check is currently manual (Phase 4a WebSearch + verify). The new verify-blog-facts.mjs (added today) needs wiring into Phase 4d
Phase 8 final report is now ~250 markdown rows — pushing email truncation limits in some clients
No GBP automation. Mondays Phase 5 generates GBP posts for ReEnergized only; the 31 microsites' GBPs are managed manually
couldBeBetter: Add Phase 4d.5 = run verify-blog-facts.mjs before deploy. If hard errors > 0, block deploy with red flag
Add Phase 2 step 7 = run check-external-duplicates.mjs. Flag <70% pages as backlog for variant-rotation rewriter
Surface estimated session time per phase in the plan output (Phase 3) so Angelo can see 'this run will be ~45min of writes'
Add 'Answer Block' insertion to Phase 4b blog template (40-60 word direct answer right after H1) — research finding from Phase 7
flaggedObsolete: Phase 0 step 7 references 'STANDING REMINDER' as plural but only Lovable Path A/B/C is currently active — text wording implies more

bail-reports.summary: Weekly bail-network snapshot. 215 domains, 3-group GSC totals, additive weekly tabs. Mature.
whatWorks: Three-group rule (Bail / Sean Sites / ReEnergized never blended) permanently fixed via hard-coded constants
Additive weekly tabs preserve history — no data lost
Every tier (S→D) shown in email body, full list in sheet tab
Time-period comparison across 5 windows surfaces month-over-month CTR trends
whatDoesntWork: Manual trigger only — easy to forget on a Monday
Full data refresh (HTTP probe + GSC multi-window) only on first Monday of month — other Mondays reuse stale data without an indicator of staleness in the email subject
couldBeBetter: Add 'data age' to email subject line: 'Weekly Report W20 (data 4d old)'
Add /bail-reports auto-suggestion to /eros-day Phase 1 every Monday (currently a Phase 1 reminder, not a trigger — keep manual but more visible)
Compare current week's tier counts to last week directly in the email body, not just the sheet

eros-report-check.summary: Audit yesterday's /eros-day report for errors + check blog cadence. Useful but secondary.
whatWorks: Catches the historical defect patterns (undefined, 0/0/0/0 rows, stale snapshots)
Blog cadence audit splits Lovable / microsites / ReEnergized correctly
Each defect classified hard/soft/expected
whatDoesntWork: Manually invoked — Angelo has to remember to run it after /eros-day
Output goes to a file, not surfaced in the next session unless Angelo opens it
couldBeBetter: Could run automatically as Phase 8.5 of /eros-day right after the final report saves
Findings could feed into operator-notes/<date>.md so next /eros-day reads them automatically

bd.summary: Update BirdsEye View. Lightweight slash command for one-off status updates.
whatWorks: Simple invocation — Angelo provides slug + status, EROS applies
whatDoesntWork: BirdsEye View blockers from todo-angelo.md never resolved (GA4 property ID wrong, Projects migration not applied, seed key missing)
No automation — every BD update is manual
couldBeBetter: Resolve the underlying BD blockers so the skill can do more than status text
Add a bulk-update mode: 'mark all microsites with status=X as live'

run-reenergized-analytics.summary: Rebuilds the 38-tab ReEnergized leads/conversion/ROI workbook. Multiple data sources, 30 cross-checks gate publish.
whatWorks: Verified every phase before publishing
30 cross-checks prevent silent data corruption
Pulls from ClubReady + Gmail + GA4 + CTM + Tidio + QuickBooks
whatDoesntWork: Long runtime — 5-10 minutes per full refresh
Requires fresh exports from ClubReady + QB to be in ~/Downloads
couldBeBetter: Pre-flight check: confirm ~/Downloads has all expected files before running
Email Sophie the freshness status weekly instead of on-demand only

analyze-data.summary: Cross-source reconciliation (QB P&L vs BQ vs Looker vs ClubReady). Read-only.
whatWorks: Read-only — can't accidentally corrupt the pipeline
Produces a Google Sheet + email per run
whatDoesntWork: Discrepancies surface but resolution still requires Sophie/Sean manual judgement
Sometimes used when /update-looker or /run-reenergized-analytics would have been more appropriate
couldBeBetter: Add a flowchart in the skill .md showing 'use this skill when X, use that skill when Y'

update-looker.summary: Weekly BQ + Looker refresh from ClubReady + QB downloads.
whatWorks: Auto-detects newest files in ~/Downloads
Dry-run before full refresh
Backup before destructive operations
whatDoesntWork: Tied to weekly Sophie cadence — if exports late, the refresh stalls
couldBeBetter: Auto-email Sophie a reminder if exports >7 days old

hug-prompt.summary: Image-to-video prompt generator tuned per video platform (HF Wan 2.2 default).
whatWorks: Per-tool tuning — different prompts for HF vs Kling vs Pixverse
Built around source-image-defines-WHAT, prompt-defines-HOW-IT-MOVES rule
whatDoesntWork: Tied to one user (creative work, not Sean business)
couldBeBetter: Out of scope for EROS — keep as is

pickleball.summary: Emmanuel's separate pickleball app workspace. Not EROS scope. Locked away from auto-trigger.
whatWorks: Explicit auto-trigger ban — 'never invoke without explicit /pickleball'
Uses angelopaulferrer-ui account (approved exception)
whatDoesntWork: Cross-contamination risk — keywords like 'app' or 'Emmanuel' could trigger it if rules slip
couldBeBetter: Move to a separate Claude profile entirely so it can't accidentally be invoked here
